cases.title: Scenario-matrix coverage for student self-scoped attendance history versus institution report denial
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 reporting/export and scope row applies from student self-service perspective (FR-37, BR-19, AC-16).
Role-matrix fixture provides Student enrolled in section A with attendance records across QR, Manual, and Admin Correction methods.
Attendance data exists for other students in the same and different sections.
steps: As authenticated Student, call GET /v1/reports/attendance with termId and classSectionId filters for an enrolled section.
Verify returned rows contain only the acting student's attendance records with status, checkInAt, and checkInMethod.
Attempt GET /v1/reports/attendance with studentUserId query targeting a different enrolled student.
Attempt POST /v1/exports/attendance with institution-wide filters as Student.
Inspect each denial response for restricted data leakage.
cases.expected: Maps to testing-plan §3.1 reporting/export and scope row: Student receives self-scoped attendance history for enrolled sections only (PRM-03); cross-student studentUserId override and institution export are denied without partial rows, export job identifiers, or other students' data disclosure (BR-19, AC-16).

cases.title: M07 report query enforces studentUserId self-scope at module and DB boundary
preconditions: Integration database seeded with attendance_records, class_sessions, enrollments, and class_sections across multiple students.
M07 Reporting module uses real repository and DB transaction boundaries with M01 scope resolution.
Student actor is enrolled in section A; foreign attendance rows exist for other students in section A and section B.
steps: Invoke M07 getAttendanceReport use-case for Student actor with filters { termId, classSectionId: sectionA }.
Assert repository query applies mandatory studentUserId = actorUserId predicate before row materialization.
Verify returned row set contains only the acting student's attendance records for enrolled section A sessions.
Confirm zero rows for other students appear even when sharing the same classSectionId.
cases.expected: Module integration returns only self-scoped attendance rows for enrolled sections; mandatory studentUserId filter enforced at M07 boundary with no cross-student leakage (PRM-03, BR-19).

cases.title: M07 student history excludes sessions for sections without active enrollment
preconditions: Integration database seeded with attendance records in section A (student enrolled) and section B (student not enrolled).
M02 enrollment boundary and M07 report query use real DB transactions.
Student actor has active enrollment only in section A per FR-04 roster.
steps: Invoke M07 getAttendanceReport for Student actor with termId spanning both sections.
Query M02 isStudentEnrolled for section B and confirm false.
Assert report result includes section A session rows only.
Verify no section B session identifiers or attendance statuses appear in committed query result.
cases.expected: Student personal history is limited to enrolled sections; unenrolled section sessions are excluded at M02/M07 boundary per BR-06 enrollment eligibility.

cases.title: Primary workflow FLOW-04 student login then views personal attendance history
preconditions: Student account with institution-issued credentials exists and is enrolled in at least one section.
Attendance records exist with Present status from QR check-in in a closed session.
No active bearer token is held by the test client.
steps: Send POST /v1/auth/login with student email and password.
Send GET /v1/me with Authorization Bearer accessToken and confirm Student role and actor identity.
Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&sortBy=date&sortOrder=desc&page=1&pageSize=25 with the student token.
Inspect data rows for session reference, attendanceStatus, checkInAt, and checkInMethod fields.
cases.expected: FLOW-04 primary path completes: authenticated student retrieves self-scoped attendance history sorted by date desc with observable status, timestamp, and method per enrolled session (FR-37).

cases.title: HTTP contract for Student POST /v1/auth/login then GET /v1/me then self-scoped attendance report
preconditions: Provisioned Student account with institution-issued credentials exists.
No active bearer token is held by the test client.
Student has attendance records in at least one enrolled section for the active term.
steps: Send POST /v1/auth/login with student email and password.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.accessToken, data.expiresInSeconds, and data.roles containing Student.
Send GET /v1/me with Authorization Bearer accessToken from login response.
Assert HTTP 200 with student actor identity and Student role scope claims.
Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=25 using the accessToken.
cases.expected: Login returns HTTP 200 with accessToken and Student role; GET /v1/me returns HTTP 200 with authenticated student identity; subsequent self-scoped GET /v1/reports/attendance returns HTTP 200 with standard envelope and only the acting student's attendance rows.

cases.title: HTTP contract for self-scoped GET /v1/reports/attendance returns status, timestamp, and check-in method
preconditions: Student is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Fixture attendance rows exist for the student with checkInMethod values QR, Manual, and Admin Correction across enrolled section sessions.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&classSectionId={sectionId}&page=1&pageSize=25 with Student Authorization header.
Validate HTTP 200 and standard { data, meta, error } response envelope.
Assert each data row includes attendanceStatus, checkInAt, checkInMethod, classSessionId, and classSectionId.
Verify checkInMethod values are drawn from allowed enum { QR, Manual, Admin Correction } per API §3.1.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 200 with standard envelope; data rows expose attendanceStatus, checkInAt, and checkInMethod for each enrolled session; error is null; methods match persisted ledger values.

cases.title: Alternate workflow branch filters personal history by section and status
preconditions: Student is authenticated and enrolled in sections A and B.
Section A has Present records; section B has Absent records for the same student in the active term.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&classSectionId={sectionAId}&page=1&pageSize=25 as Student.
Record returned row count and section identifiers.
Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&status=Absent&page=1&pageSize=25 as Student.
Assert filtered results contain only Absent rows for the acting student within enrolled scope.
cases.expected: Alternate filter branch returns section-scoped and status-scoped subsets of the student's own records only; no rows from unfiltered institution scope or other students appear.

cases.title: Student personal history pagination returns scoped totalItems and page boundaries
preconditions: Student actor is authenticated in integration harness.
More than 25 attendance records exist for the student in one enrolled section (exceeding default pageSize).
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&classSectionId={sectionId}&page=1&pageSize=25 as Student.
Assert meta.pagination contains page=1, pageSize=25, totalItems, and totalPages reflecting only the student's scoped count.
Request page=2 with same filters and verify non-overlapping row identifiers.
Confirm totalItems does not include other students' record counts (NFR-LST-01).
cases.expected: meta.pagination reflects self-scoped totalItems only; page 1 and page 2 return disjoint row sets; scoped count never exposes cross-student or institution-wide totals.

cases.title: Student denied when querying another student's attendance via studentUserId override
preconditions: Two Student accounts exist: studentA (authenticated) and studentB with attendance records in the same section.
studentB attendance data exists and is known to the test harness.
steps: Authenticate as studentA and obtain Bearer token.
Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&studentUserId={studentBUserId}&page=1&pageSize=25.
Validate HTTP status code and error envelope.
Inspect data and meta.pagination for any studentB attendance rows, identifiers, or scoped totals.
cases.expected: Request returns HTTP 403 with error.code Forbidden or OutOfScope; data is null; no studentB attendance statuses, timestamps, methods, or pagination totals are disclosed (PRM-03, BR-19).

cases.title: Student denied institution-wide CSV export
preconditions: Student user is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Attendance export data exists across multiple sections and students.
steps: Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with body { format: csv, filters: { termId } } and required Idempotency-Key as Student.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON response envelope.
Verify no exportJobId is returned and no export job is created in persistence.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 403 with standard envelope; error.code is Forbidden; data is null with no exportJobId or queued job metadata disclosed (FR-37 export denial, AC-16).

cases.title: Student denied live session roster endpoint exposing other students
preconditions: Student is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Open class session exists where the student is enrolled with peer students checked in.
steps: Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance as Student.
Validate HTTP status code and error envelope.
Inspect response for peer student identifiers, attendance statuses, or roster counts.
cases.expected: Request returns HTTP 403 with error.code Forbidden or OutOfScope; data is null; no peer student roster rows or live attendance counts are disclosed — personal history remains on self-scoped report path only (FR-19 boundary, BR-19).

cases.title: Student history pageSize above maximum 100 rejected
preconditions: Student is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Pagination constraint: 1 <= pageSize <= 100 per API §2.5 and validation rules.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=101 as Student.
Validate HTTP status and error envelope.
Confirm no attendance rows are returned in data array for invalid pageSize.
cases.expected: pageSize=101 returns HTTP 400 with InvalidPayload or validation error; data is null with no partial attendance history rows.

cases.title: Unauthenticated personal attendance history request rejected
preconditions: Student attendance records exist in the database.
Request is sent without Authorization header.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=25 without Authorization header.
Inspect HTTP status, response envelope data/meta/error fields.
Verify no attendance rows or pagination metadata are returned.
cases.expected: API returns HTTP 401 with standard envelope where data is null and error.code is Unauthenticated; no attendance history rows or scoped totals are disclosed.

cases.title: Student browser journey views PG-03 attendance history with filters, badges, and no export
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile-web Chromium viewport.
PG-03 /me/attendance route is reachable with AttendanceHistoryList (DC-10) component.
Student has attendance records across multiple enrolled sections with Present, Late, and Absent statuses.
steps: Navigate to /me/attendance and confirm history list loads with section grouping and AttendanceStatusCell badges.
Apply termId and classSectionId filters via toolbar or bottom sheet; verify table updates to scoped rows only.
Confirm each row displays check-in timestamp and method badge (QR, Manual, or Admin Correction).
Verify no export button or ExportScopeSummary affordance is present on the page.
Navigate to page 2 via pagination controls and confirm URL query params sync (termId, classSectionId, status, sortBy, sortOrder, page, pageSize).
cases.expected: PG-03 listing implements mobile-first self-scoped history per §0 matrix: filters, date desc sort, server pagination, status and method columns visible; no export affordance; displayed rows remain strictly the acting student's records (PRM-03, FR-37).

cases.title: Browser journey blocks student from institution report route and shows empty or access-denied state
preconditions: Student test account is logged in on browser harness.
PG-13 /reports/attendance route exists for lecturer and admin roles.
Institution-wide attendance data exists outside student self-scope.
steps: As Student, attempt direct URL navigation to /reports/attendance.
Observe permission empty, redirect, or access-denied state without partial attendance table data.
Confirm /me/attendance remains reachable and shows only self-scoped records.
Verify no institution-wide row counts or other students' identifiers appear on either route.
cases.expected: Student is denied institution report UI without restricted data leakage; personal history at /me/attendance remains the authorized self-service path (AC-16, BR-19, AC-UI-09).
